translations.title: Git tags cho release management như thế nào?
translations.content: Giải thích cách sử dụng Git tags để quản lý releases.
translations.answer:
**Tag Types**:

1. **Lightweight tags** (just a pointer):
```bash
git tag v1.0.0
```

2. **Annotated tags** (recommended for releases):
```bash
git tag -a v1.0.0 -m "Release version 1.0.0"
```

**Creating Tags**:
```bash
# Tag current commit
git tag -a v1.0.0 -m "Release 1.0.0"

# Tag specific commit
git tag -a v1.0.0 abc1234 -m "Release 1.0.0"

# Tag with GPG signature
git tag -s v1.0.0 -m "Signed release 1.0.0"
```

**Listing Tags**:
```bash
git tag                    # List all tags
git tag -l "v1.*"          # Filter tags
git show v1.0.0            # Show tag details
git tag -l --sort=-v:refname  # Sort by version
```

**Pushing Tags**:
```bash
git push origin v1.0.0     # Push specific tag
git push origin --tags     # Push all tags
git push --follow-tags     # Push commits and annotated tags
```

**Deleting Tags**:
```bash
git tag -d v1.0.0          # Delete local
git push origin :refs/tags/v1.0.0  # Delete remote
git push origin --delete v1.0.0    # Delete remote (alt)
```

**Semantic Versioning**:
```
MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
v1.0.0 → v1.0.1 (patch: bug fixes)
v1.0.1 → v1.1.0 (minor: new features)
v1.1.0 → v2.0.0 (major: breaking changes)
```

**Release Workflow**:
```bash
# 1. Update version in package.json
npm version patch  # or minor/major

# 2. This creates commit and tag automatically
# 3. Push with tags
git push --follow-tags

# 4. Create GitHub release from tag
gh release create v1.0.0 --generate-notes
```

**Checkout Tag**:
```bash
git checkout v1.0.0        # Detached HEAD
git checkout -b hotfix v1.0.0  # New branch from tag
```

translations.title: Git bisect để tìm commit gây bug như thế nào?
translations.content: Giải thích cách sử dụng git bisect để tìm commit đã introduce bug.
translations.answer:
**Basic Bisect**:
```bash
# Start bisect
git bisect start

# Mark current (broken) as bad
git bisect bad

# Mark known good commit
git bisect good v1.0.0

# Git checks out middle commit
# Test and mark
git bisect good  # or bad

# Repeat until found
# Git shows: "abc123 is the first bad commit"

# End bisect
git bisect reset
```

**Automated Bisect**:
```bash
# Run script to determine good/bad
git bisect start HEAD v1.0.0
git bisect run npm test

# Script exit codes:
# 0 = good
# 1-124, 126-127 = bad
# 125 = skip (can't test this commit)
```

**Custom Test Script**:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# test-bug.sh

npm install --silent
if npm test 2>&1 | grep -q "specific error"; then
  exit 1  # bad
else
  exit 0  # good
fi

# Run
git bisect run ./test-bug.sh
```

**Skip Commits**:
```bash
# If commit can't be tested (broken build)
git bisect skip

# Skip range
git bisect skip v1.0.1..v1.0.5
```

**View Progress**:
```bash
git bisect log    # Show bisect history
git bisect visualize  # Open in gitk
```

**Example Session**:
```bash
$ git bisect start
$ git bisect bad HEAD
$ git bisect good v2.0.0
Bisecting: 10 revisions left to test
[abc123] Add feature X

$ npm test
# Tests pass
$ git bisect good
Bisecting: 5 revisions left to test

$ npm test
# Tests fail
$ git bisect bad
Bisecting: 2 revisions left to test

# Continue until:
abc123 is the first bad commit

$ git bisect reset
```

**Tips**:
- Start with known good và bad commits
- Use automated scripts khi có thể
- Check compile/build success trước khi test

translations.title: Git diff hiệu quả và tạo/apply patches như thế nào?
translations.content: Giải thích cách sử dụng git diff và tạo/apply patches.
translations.answer:
**Basic Diff**:
```bash
# Working directory vs staged
git diff

# Staged vs last commit
git diff --staged
git diff --cached

# Between commits
git diff abc123..def456
git diff HEAD~3..HEAD

# Specific file
git diff -- path/to/file.ts
```

**Diff Options**:
```bash
# Word diff (better for prose)
git diff --word-diff

# Stats only
git diff --stat

# Name only
git diff --name-only
git diff --name-status

# Ignore whitespace
git diff -w
git diff --ignore-all-space

# Context lines
git diff -U5  # 5 lines context (default 3)
```

**Compare Branches**:
```bash
# Changes in feature not in main
git diff main...feature

# Changes since branch point
git diff $(git merge-base main feature)..feature
```

**Creating Patches**:
```bash
# Single commit
git format-patch -1 HEAD

# Range of commits
git format-patch main..feature

# Output to directory
git format-patch -o patches/ main..feature

# Simple diff patch
git diff > changes.patch
git diff --staged > staged.patch
```

**Applying Patches**:
```bash
# Apply format-patch output
git am 0001-Add-feature.patch
git am patches/*.patch

# Apply diff patch
git apply changes.patch

# Check if patch applies
git apply --check changes.patch

# Apply with 3-way merge
git am -3 patch.patch
```

**Interactive Patch**:
```bash
# Stage hunks interactively
git add -p

# Unstage hunks
git reset -p

# Checkout hunks
git checkout -p
```

**Useful Aliases**:
```bash
git config --global alias.d "diff --word-diff"
git config --global alias.ds "diff --staged"
git config --global alias.dc "diff --cached"
```

translations.title: Sử dụng git stash hiệu quả như thế nào?
translations.content: Giải thích cách sử dụng git stash để quản lý work-in-progress changes.
translations.answer:
**Basic Stash**:
```bash
# Stash working directory và staged changes
git stash
git stash push

# Stash với message
git stash push -m "WIP: feature X"

# Stash including untracked files
git stash -u
git stash --include-untracked

# Stash including ignored files
git stash -a
git stash --all
```

**Listing Stashes**:
```bash
git stash list
# stash@{0}: WIP on main: abc123 message
# stash@{1}: On feature: def456 another message

git stash show        # Show diff stats
git stash show -p     # Show full diff
git stash show stash@{1}
```

**Applying Stashes**:
```bash
# Apply most recent, keep in stash
git stash apply

# Apply and remove from stash
git stash pop

# Apply specific stash
git stash apply stash@{2}
git stash pop stash@{2}
```

**Selective Stashing**:
```bash
# Stash specific files
git stash push -m "message" -- file1.ts file2.ts

# Interactive stash (select hunks)
git stash -p
```

**Managing Stashes**:
```bash
# Drop specific stash
git stash drop stash@{1}

# Clear all stashes
git stash clear

# Create branch from stash
git stash branch new-feature stash@{0}
```

**Stash Workflow Example**:
```bash
# Working on feature, urgent bug reported
git stash push -m "WIP: feature X"

# Fix bug
git checkout -b hotfix
# ... make fixes ...
git commit -m "Fix critical bug"
git checkout main
git merge hotfix

# Return to feature
git checkout feature
git stash pop
```

**Tips**:
- Use descriptive messages: `git stash push -m "description"`
- Don't let stashes pile up — apply or drop regularly
- `git stash branch` is useful for complex stashes

translations.title: Interactive rebase để clean up commit history như thế nào?
translations.content: Giải thích cách sử dụng interactive rebase để clean up commits trước khi merge.
translations.answer:
**Start Interactive Rebase**:
```bash
git rebase -i HEAD~5     # Last 5 commits
git rebase -i main       # Since branching from main
git rebase -i abc123     # Since specific commit
```

**Editor Commands**:
```
pick abc1234 First commit
pick def5678 Second commit
pick ghi9012 Third commit

# Commands:
# p, pick = use commit
# r, reword = use commit, edit message
# e, edit = use commit, stop for amending
# s, squash = meld into previous, keep message
# f, fixup = meld into previous, discard message
# d, drop = remove commit
# x, exec = run command
```

**Common Operations**:

1. **Squash commits**:
```
pick abc1234 Add feature
squash def5678 Fix typo
squash ghi9012 More fixes
# Results in one commit with combined messages
```

2. **Fixup** (squash without message):
```
pick abc1234 Add feature
fixup def5678 Fix typo
fixup ghi9012 More fixes
# Results in one commit with first message only
```

3. **Reorder commits**:
```
# Before:
pick abc1234 Feature A
pick def5678 Feature B
pick ghi9012 Fix for A

# After (move fix next to feature):
pick abc1234 Feature A
pick ghi9012 Fix for A
pick def5678 Feature B
```

4. **Edit commit**:
```
edit abc1234 Commit to modify
pick def5678 Next commit

# Git stops at abc1234
git add/rm files
git commit --amend
git rebase --continue
```

**Autosquash**:
```bash
# Create fixup commit
git commit --fixup abc1234

# Later, auto-squash
git rebase -i --autosquash main
```

**Abort/Continue**:
```bash
git rebase --abort      # Cancel rebase
git rebase --continue   # After resolving conflicts
git rebase --skip       # Skip current commit
```

**Best Practice**: Clean up commits before PR merge, not after.

translations.title: .gitignore hoạt động như thế nào và các patterns phổ biến?
translations.content: Giải thích cách .gitignore hoạt động và các patterns thường dùng.
translations.answer:
**Basic Patterns**:
```gitignore
# Ignore file
file.txt

# Ignore directory
node_modules/

# Wildcard
*.log
*.tmp

# Double asterisk (any directory depth)
**/logs
logs/**/debug.log

# Negation (include)
!important.log

# Character range
[0-9].txt
```

**Common .gitignore**:
```gitignore
# Dependencies
node_modules/

# Build output
dist/
build/
.next/

# Environment
.env
.env.local
.env*.local

# IDE
.idea/
.vscode/
*.swp

# OS
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# Logs
*.log
logs/

# Coverage
coverage/

# Cache
.cache/
.turbo/
```

**Gitignore Levels**:
1. **Repository**: `.gitignore` in repo
2. **Global**: `~/.gitignore_global`
3. **Local**: `.git/info/exclude`

**Setup Global Gitignore**:
```bash
git config --global core.excludesFile ~/.gitignore_global

# ~/.gitignore_global
.DS_Store
.idea/
*.swp
```

**Ignore Already Tracked Files**:
```bash
# Remove from tracking, keep file
git rm --cached file.txt
git rm -r --cached directory/

# Then add to .gitignore
echo "file.txt" >> .gitignore
```

**Check What's Ignored**:
```bash
git status --ignored
git check-ignore -v file.txt
```

**Force Add Ignored File**:
```bash
git add -f ignored-file.txt
```

**Template .gitignore**:
```bash
# Use GitHub's templates
curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/gitignore/main/Node.gitignore > .gitignore

# Or gitignore.io
curl gitignore.io/api/node,react > .gitignore
```

translations.title: Git lưu trữ data internally như thế nào? Giải thích blobs, trees, và commits.
translations.content: Giải thích cách Git lưu trữ data với blobs, trees, và commits.
translations.answer:
**Git Object Types**:

1. **Blob** — File content:
```bash
# Store content, get SHA-1
echo "Hello" | git hash-object -w --stdin
# Returns: ce013625030ba8dba906f756967f9e9ca394464a

# View blob
git cat-file -p ce0136
# Output: Hello
```

2. **Tree** — Directory structure:
```bash
git cat-file -p HEAD^{tree}
# 100644 blob abc123    README.md
# 040000 tree def456    src
```

3. **Commit** — Snapshot with metadata:
```bash
git cat-file -p HEAD
# tree abc123
# parent def456
# author Name <email> timestamp
# committer Name <email> timestamp
#
# Commit message
```

**Object Storage**:
```
.git/objects/
├── ce/
│   └── 013625030ba8dba906f756967f9e9ca394464a
├── pack/
│   ├── pack-abc123.idx
│   └── pack-abc123.pack
└── info/
```

**How Commits Link**:
```
Commit (HEAD)
  ↓ tree
Tree (root)
  ↓ blob, tree
Blob (file content)
```

**References**:
```bash
# Branches are files pointing to commits
cat .git/refs/heads/main
# abc123def456...

# HEAD points to current branch
cat .git/HEAD
# ref: refs/heads/main
```

**Useful Commands**:
```bash
# Count objects
git count-objects -v

# Verify integrity
git fsck

# View object type
git cat-file -t abc123

# View object content
git cat-file -p abc123

# Show all refs
git show-ref
```

**Pack Files**:
- Git packs loose objects periodically
- Delta compression between similar objects
- `.pack` contains compressed objects
- `.idx` is index for fast lookup

```bash
# Manual pack
git gc

# Verify pack
git verify-pack -v .git/objects/pack/pack-*.idx
```

translations.title: Git hooks là gì và dùng trong team như thế nào?
translations.content: Giải thích Git hooks và cách sử dụng chúng trong team development.
translations.answer:
**Git Hooks Location**:
```
.git/hooks/
├── pre-commit.sample
├── pre-push.sample
├── commit-msg.sample
└── ...
```

**Common Hooks**:

1. **pre-commit** — Before commit:
```bash
#!/bin/sh
npm run lint
npm run test
```

2. **commit-msg** — Validate message:
```bash
#!/bin/sh
if ! grep -qE "^(feat|fix|docs|style|refactor|test|chore): .+" "$1"; then
  echo "Invalid commit message format"
  exit 1
fi
```

3. **pre-push** — Before push:
```bash
#!/bin/sh
npm run test:all
```

**Sharing Hooks with Husky**:
```bash
npm install husky -D
npx husky init
```

```json
// package.json
{
  "scripts": {
    "prepare": "husky"
  }
}
```

```bash
# .husky/pre-commit
npm run lint-staged

# .husky/commit-msg
npx --no -- commitlint --edit $1
```

**lint-staged** — Only lint staged files:
```json
// package.json
{
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.{ts,tsx}": ["eslint --fix", "prettier --write"],
    "*.{json,md}": "prettier --write"
  }
}
```

**commitlint** — Enforce commit message format:
```js
// commitlint.config.js
module.exports = {
  extends: ['@commitlint/config-conventional'],
};
```

**Server-side Hooks**:
- `pre-receive` — Before accepting push
- `post-receive` — After push (CI/CD trigger)
- `update` — Per-branch validation

**Skip Hooks** (use carefully):
```bash
git commit --no-verify
git push --no-verify
```

**Best Practices**:
- Keep hooks fast (<5 seconds)
- Use lint-staged for large repos
- Share via Husky or similar
- Document bypass procedure

translations.title: Git worktrees là gì và khi nào hữu ích?
translations.content: Giải thích git worktrees và các use cases của chúng.
translations.answer:
**Git Worktree là gì?**
- Multiple working directories từ một repository
- Share `.git` folder
- Work on multiple branches simultaneously

**Basic Commands**:
```bash
# Create worktree
git worktree add ../project-hotfix hotfix-branch

# Create with new branch
git worktree add -b feature-x ../project-feature main

# List worktrees
git worktree list

# Remove worktree
git worktree remove ../project-hotfix

# Prune stale worktrees
git worktree prune
```

**Directory Structure**:
```
~/projects/
├── my-project/           # Main worktree
│   ├── .git/
│   └── src/
├── my-project-hotfix/    # Worktree for hotfix
│   └── src/
└── my-project-feature/   # Worktree for feature
    └── src/
```

**Use Cases**:

1. **Urgent hotfix while working on feature**:
```bash
# Currently on feature branch
git worktree add ../hotfix main
cd ../hotfix
git checkout -b hotfix-123
# Fix bug, commit, push
cd ../main-project
git worktree remove ../hotfix
```

2. **Compare implementations**:
```bash
git worktree add ../compare-old old-implementation
git worktree add ../compare-new new-implementation
# Open both in editors, compare side-by-side
```

3. **Run tests on different branches**:
```bash
git worktree add ../test-main main
cd ../test-main && npm test &
cd ../feature && npm test &
```

4. **Long-running tasks**:
```bash
# Build production while developing
git worktree add ../build-prod production
cd ../build-prod && npm run build
```

**Limitations**:
- Can't checkout same branch in multiple worktrees
- Each worktree needs own node_modules

**vs. git clone**:
| Feature | Worktree | Clone |
|---------|----------|-------|
| Disk space | Shared .git | Separate |
| Fetch once | Yes | No |
| Branch lock | Yes | No |

translations.title: Best practices cho tạo và review pull requests?
translations.content: Giải thích best practices khi tạo và review pull requests.
translations.answer:
**Creating PRs**:

1. **Small, focused PRs**:
```
# Good: One feature/fix per PR
# Bad: Multiple unrelated changes
```

2. **Clear title và description**:
```markdown
## Summary
- Add user authentication
- Implement JWT tokens

## Changes
- Added auth middleware
- Created login/logout endpoints
- Added user session management

## Testing
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] Manual testing done

## Screenshots (if UI changes)
```

3. **Self-review first**:
```bash
# Review your own diff
git diff main...feature

# Check for:
# - Debug code
# - Console.logs
# - Commented code
# - Sensitive data
```

4. **Clean commit history**:
```bash
# Squash WIP commits
git rebase -i main
```

**Reviewing PRs**:

1. **Understand context first**:
- Read description
- Check linked issues
- Understand the "why"

2. **Review systematically**:
```
1. Architecture/design
2. Logic/correctness
3. Error handling
4. Tests
5. Code style
```

3. **Constructive feedback**:
```markdown
# Good
"Consider using `useMemo` here to avoid recalculating on every render"

# Bad
"This is wrong"
```

4. **Use suggestions**:
```markdown
```suggestion
const value = useMemo(() => expensiveCalc(data), [data]);
```
```

**PR Checklist**:
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] No merge conflicts
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] No console.logs
- [ ] Error handling
- [ ] Security considerations

**GitHub CLI**:
```bash
# Create PR
gh pr create --title "Add feature" --body "Description"

# Review PR
gh pr checkout 123
gh pr review 123 --approve
gh pr merge 123 --squash
```

translations.title: Git cherry-pick là gì và khi nào nên dùng?
translations.content: Giải thích git cherry-pick và các use cases của nó.
translations.answer:
**Cherry-pick là gì?**
- Apply specific commit(s) từ branch này sang branch khác
- Creates new commit(s) với same changes

**Basic Usage**:
```bash
# Pick single commit
git cherry-pick abc123

# Pick multiple commits
git cherry-pick abc123 def456 ghi789

# Pick range
git cherry-pick abc123..def456  # Exclusive
git cherry-pick abc123^..def456 # Inclusive
```

**Options**:
```bash
# Don't commit (stage changes only)
git cherry-pick -n abc123
git cherry-pick --no-commit abc123

# Edit commit message
git cherry-pick -e abc123

# Append cherry-pick info to message
git cherry-pick -x abc123
# Adds: (cherry picked from commit abc123)
```

**Use Cases**:

1. **Hotfix from develop to main**:
```bash
git checkout main
git cherry-pick abc123  # Bug fix commit from develop
```

2. **Backport feature to older version**:
```bash
git checkout release-1.0
git cherry-pick feature-commit
```

3. **Extract commit from wrong branch**:
```bash
# Accidentally committed to main
git checkout feature-branch
git cherry-pick abc123

# Remove from main
git checkout main
git reset --hard HEAD~1
```

**Handling Conflicts**:
```bash
git cherry-pick abc123
# CONFLICT

# Resolve conflicts
git add resolved-file.ts
git cherry-pick --continue

# Or abort
git cherry-pick --abort
```

**When NOT to use**:
- Many commits (use merge/rebase instead)
- Already merged branches (duplicates)
- Public commits (creates duplicate history)

**Best Practices**:
- Use `-x` để track source commit
- Cherry-pick oldest first (sequential)
- Prefer merge/rebase for full branches

translations.title: Git reflog là gì và làm sao có thể cứu bạn khỏi mistakes?
translations.content: Giải thích git reflog và cách dùng nó để recover từ mistakes.
translations.answer:
**Reflog là gì?**
- Log mọi thay đổi của HEAD và branch refs
- Local only (không push)
- Kept for 90 days by default
- Your safety net!

**View Reflog**:
```bash
git reflog
# abc123 HEAD@{0}: commit: Add feature
# def456 HEAD@{1}: checkout: moving from main to feature
# ghi789 HEAD@{2}: reset: moving to HEAD~1

# Specific branch
git reflog show feature

# With dates
git reflog --date=iso
```

**Recovery Scenarios**:

1. **Undo hard reset**:
```bash
# Oops!
git reset --hard HEAD~3

# Recovery
git reflog
# Find commit before reset: abc123 HEAD@{1}
git reset --hard abc123
```

2. **Recover deleted branch**:
```bash
# Oops!
git branch -D feature

# Recovery
git reflog
# Find last commit on feature: def456 HEAD@{5}
git checkout -b feature def456
```

3. **Undo bad rebase**:
```bash
# Oops! Rebase went wrong
git rebase --abort  # If still in progress

# Or find pre-rebase state
git reflog
# ghi789 HEAD@{10}: rebase (start)
git reset --hard HEAD@{10}
```

4. **Find lost commit**:
```bash
# Commit exists but not in any branch
git reflog | grep "commit message"
git checkout abc123
git branch recovered-work
```

**Reflog Reference Syntax**:
```bash
HEAD@{0}      # Current HEAD
HEAD@{1}      # Previous HEAD
main@{1}      # Previous main
HEAD@{yesterday}
HEAD@{2.hours.ago}
```

**Config**:
```bash
# Change expiry (default 90 days)
git config gc.reflogExpire 180.days
git config gc.reflogExpireUnreachable 90.days
```

**Tips**:
- Check reflog before panicking
- `git fsck --lost-found` for dangling commits
- Reflog is local — won't help with remote issues
